title: Ram Teerath
tagline: Ram Teerath — spiritual town of Punjab

overview.short: Bhagwan Valmiki Tirath Asthan is temple panorama complex and an important historical monument of Valmikis located at Bhagwan Valmiki Tirath road of Amritsar city.
overview.description: Bhagwan Valmiki Tirath Asthan is temple panorama complex and an important historical monument of Valmikis located at Bhagwan Valmiki Tirath road of Amritsar city. Since 1 December 2016, it has an 8-foot-tall 800-kg gold-plated idol of Sage Valmiki in main section. Formerly known as Ram Tirath, the location was renamed after Balmiki in 2016, a sign of the successful appropriation and reclamation of the site by the Balmiki community.

nearestAirport.name: Sri Guru Ram Dass Jee International Airport, Amritsar

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Indian Airlines Flight 427
topPlaces.description: Indian Airlines Flight 427 was a domestic passenger flight of the Indian Airlines between Srinagar Airport and the Delhi-Indira Gandhi International Airport. The aircraft was involved in an aircraft hijacking that took place in India between 24 and 25 April 1993.

topPlaces.name: Bhittewadh
topPlaces.description: Bhittewadh is a village in Batala in Gurdaspur district of Punjab State, India. The village is administrated by Sarpanch an elected representative of the village.

topPlaces.name: Chheharta Sahib
topPlaces.description: Chheharta Sahib is a town located 7 km west of Amritsar city of Punjab in India, on the Grand Trunk Road leading to the Pakistan border. The town derives its name from the Gurudwara Chheharta Sahib, established by the fifth Sikh Guru Guru Arjan Dev.

topPlaces.name: Rajasansi
topPlaces.description: Rajasansi is a town, near Amritsar city and a nagar panchayat in Amritsar district in the Indian state of Punjab. Sri Guru Ram Dass Jee International Airport (Amritsar International Airport) is located in Rajasansi village on Ajnala-Rajasansi Road.

topPlaces.name: Amritsar Cantonment
topPlaces.description: Amritsar Cantonment is a cantonment town in Amritsar District in the state of Punjab, India.

topPlaces.name: Harsha Chhina
topPlaces.description: Harsha Chhina is a village in the state of Punjab, India, three kilometers from Sri Guru Ram Dass Jee International Airport, Amritsar, on Ajnala Road. Harse is an Urdu word which means three villages (Uchla Quila, Vichla Quila, and Sabajpura).

topPlaces.name: Sikh History Research Centre
topPlaces.description: The management committee of Khalsa College Amritsar created the Sikh History Research Centre in 1930 to write and publish the Sikh history from a modern historical perspective.

topPlaces.name: Adliwala
topPlaces.description: Adliwala village is located in Ajnala tehsil in the Amritsar district of Punjab, 16 km away from the district headquarters of Amritsar. As of 2011, the total population of the village was 3,949 (2,122 men and 1,827 women) living in 849 households.

itinerary.title: Day 3 — Sikh History Research Centre & around

items.note: The management committee of Khalsa College Amritsar created the Sikh History Research Centre in 1930 to write and publish the Sikh history from a modern historical perspective.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Ram Teerath?
faq.a: The best time to visit Ram Teerath is Oct – Mar. Summers are 28–44°C and winters are 8–25°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Ram Teerath?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Sri Guru Ram Dass Jee International Airport, Amritsar (6 km) and the nearest railway station is Amritsar Junction (11 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Ram Teerath?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Ram Teerath at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Ram Teerath?
faq.a: Stays in Ram Teerath start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Ram Teerath?
faq.a: The top attractions are Indian Airlines Flight 427, Bhittewadh, Chheharta Sahib, Rajasansi, Amritsar Cantonment.
